text: Refactoring means rewriting code to improve its structure without changing what it does. The goal is to make the code clearer, more efficient, and easier to maintain. It’s like tidying up a messy room—you rearrange things so it’s easier to find what you need, but you don’t add or remove furniture. Good refactoring focuses on readability, reducing duplication, and simplifying complex logic. This practice helps prevent technical debt, which is the extra work caused by poorly designed code. By regularly refactoring, developers save time in the long run and create software that is easier to update or fix.
